summary: Modifying the RTL VScrollBar step causes scrolling to stop working w/ large font sizes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5.1 and 4.6 dev4

### System information

Any

### Issue description

Changing the scroll step works on small font sizes, but breaks with large ones.
```GDScript
var v_scroll_bar := rich_text_label.get_v_scroll_bar()
v_scroll_bar.step = rich_text_label.get_line_height(0)
```

This will correctly scroll full lines (assuming even line height throughout) using the default font size, but will break when the font size exceeds 27-30. It will still work if you hover over the scroll bar when scrolling, but won't when hovering over the RTL as a whole.

Accounting for uneven sizes between the label `size.y` and the scroll bar's `step`/`max_value` doesn't have any effect.

Setting the Focus mode to All and scrolling with the arrow keys works fine.

The RTL registers inputs as usual, it simply seems not to pass the scroll events to the VScrollBar

### Steps to reproduce

Run MRP.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[rtl-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23779304/rtl-bug.zip)

summary: Support MultiMesh objects in the scene Advanced Import Settings dialog

description.text: This PR updates the editor scene Advanced Import Settings dialog to support MultiMeshInstance3D objects, displaying the Mesh resource inside their MultiMesh resource in the tree on the left: This PR also unifies the `accum_xform` calculation since it is used in 3 different cases now. The performance of this code is not important because this is editor-only code, so calculating a potentially unused transform is not problematic. The scene importers currently do not have any code to bring in MultiMeshInstance3D nodes, but it is possible to do with extensions/plugins, and this will be a built-in feature after PR #112866.

summary: Relative Mouse Motion Regression from 4.5 to 4.5.1

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.5.1.stable, 4.6 (dev 4)
- Not reproducable in 4.5.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (ADL GT2) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1240P (16 threads) - 15.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

Values of `InputEventMouseMotion:relative`, have become jumpy and inconsistent (frame time is not affected, but the relative motion value "jumps" frequently even if mouse motion is smooth and consistent). In 4.5.stable this issue is not present, and seems to have been introduced in 4.5.1.stable, though I could not pinpoint an exact commit yet.

To keep things simple, MRP was tested with a 60hz display, vsync, and no interpolation, though the bug still occurs with any variation of these settings I've tried.

~~It's possible this might be fixed by #112515 seeing as it relates to eating mouse movement events and I am on X11, though this bug is present regardless of the `Input.use_accumulated_input` setting.~~

### Steps to reproduce

Open MRP, run the main scene, and press space bar to smoothly move the mouse in a circle.
- In 4.5.stable, movement between the 2 test meshes should be buttery smooth
- In 4.5.1.stable onward, you should see very choppy motion between the 2 test meshes (multiple "jumps" per second)

summary: Use new dock system for Shader Editor Dock

description.text:
See #113024

Upgraded the Shader Editor Dock to use the new system. I also added a `make_dock_floating()` function in `EditorDockManager` to force a dock to be floating through code.

Now that this is a dock, I removed the internal floating logic and button to use the Dock methods. The dock has to be made floating by right-clicking the tab or using the shortcut. Since all docks should be made floating this is standard behavior.

My primary motive in removing the floating button is leaving the door open to refactors that might make individual shader file editors docks (ie able to edit multiple shaders on screen at once). Leaving the button in would necessitate moving almost the entirety of the plugin into a new class extending dock, which would have to be reversed if it were refactored to edit multiple shaders on screen at once.

I also reordered the constructor to be more in line with the rest of the editor.

summary: Update the documentation for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` & add error messages.

description.text: TL;DR - Updates the documentation for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` - Adds error messages for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` - Closes #103101 This PR updates the documentation for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` & tells the user that all 3 methods can fail during mouse selection events. It also adds error messages for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` The user will get the following error message if attempting to use any of the 3 methods during mouse selection events (Following @RedMser's comment in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105969#pullrequestreview-2810059679): (This is a test using the script provided by @kleonc in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103101#issuecomment-2673123694) Text version: ``` E 0:00:02:715 tree.gd:14 @ _on_item_selected(): The tree cannot create items during mouse selection events. Condition "blocked > 0" is true. Returning: nullptr scene\gui\tree.cpp:5188 @ Tree::create_item() tree.gd:14 @ _on_item_selected() ``` (We use `ERR_FAIL_COND_V_MSG` instead of `ERR_FAIL_COND_MSG` here because Godot expects us to return a value or else we get build errors. We just return `nullptr` in this case.) ### MRP: You can test with [this](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23776082/113178-mrp.zip) If you face any issues while testing, feel free to ping me. summary: Editor can't infer the class of a custom resource with a non-`class_name`-line script while quick loading.

description.text: ### Tested versions -Reproducible in: 4.5.1-stable ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable (f62fdbde1) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon (TM) 780M Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21001.9024) - AMD Ryzen 7 7840H w/ Radeon(TM) 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 31.30 GiB memory ### Issue description I have a custom class, `CustomRes`, extends `Resource`. Then I created 2 resources, in one of which I changed its script to a different new one(with some methods overrided). The new script states `extends CustomRes`, but doesn't has a `class_name` line. When I try to quick load the custom resources in a scene with `@export var custom_res : CustomRes`, the editor only provides me with the script-unchanged CustomRes resource. The resource with a different script is ignored. However, the resource with a different script can still be dragged into the export custom_res slot. --- I think this is not good. The new script clearly declared it extends from the origin class, but the resource with it failed to get recognized in the editor's quick load. I expect the custom resource with a new script (declaring it extends to the origin class) can still get inferred by the editor and can be quick loaded. This could be useful when I create many custom resources while some of them have special scripts for specific functions, and thus these custom resources can be equally treated when quick loaded. ### Steps to reproduce Open the mrp project. Open test.tscn, and try `Quick Load` of `Custom Res` in the Inspector ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [mrp-resource-class-inferring.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23762392/mrp-resource-class-inferring.zip)

summary: Setting default value in a custom resource to the same value as an override behaves differently if the resource is stored in a separate file or in the scene

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1]

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7242) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.93 GiB memory

### Issue description

When changing the default value in a custom resource script to be the same as in an existing resource instance, then this will cause a resource that's stored inside a scene to adopt the default value and keep changing with further changes of the default value, while a resource that's stored in a separate file will remember its override and stay at the same value if the default value is changed.

I would expect there to be no difference in behaviour for one or the other.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a custom resource with an export property and default value (e.g. var health = 10) and a scene with a node that takes two of this resource. Make one of them an inline resource and the other a resource stored in a .tres file and set the value for both to something different than the default (e.g. both to 20). (starting point for MRP .zip)
2. change the default value in the resource script to that same value (make it "var health = 20")
3. see in the inspector that both resources indicate their value is 20 and no circular "value reset" arrow is present, indicating this is the same as the default value
4. run the program to verify (or maybe just pressing Ctrl + S is enough at this point)
5. change the default value in the resource script to another value (e.g. "var health = 40")
(Please note that at this point both resouces will show that their value is 40, however that's a visual bug, separately documented in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/113169)
(6. as long as issue #113169 is unresolved, select "Reload Current Project" to make the inspector display the correct values)
7. see in the inspector that the resource that was stored in the scene has now health = 40, while the resource that is stored in the separate .tres file still has health = 20

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Open main.tscn and follow the steps outlined above.
[resource-default-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23757514/resource-default-issue.zip)

summary: Changing Default value of Resource script to the same value as override causes inspector to show wrong value

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1]

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7242) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.93 GiB memory

### Issue description

When I create a custom resource with a custom property (e.g. "health") and change the default value in the script to be the same as what one instance of the resource has it overridden to, then the inspector will indicate that the resource is using the default value, even though it is not. Since when changing the default value in the script again, the value in the resource instance will stay the same as it always was.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a custom resource with a property and a resource file that overrides that property 
(e.g. EnemyComponent with health property with default value = 10, and an enemy1.tres with health = 30) (starting point for MRP .zip)
2. Change the default value of this property in the resource script to be the same value as the resource instance (e.g. health = 30)
3. Look at the inspector for the resource instance, it tells me the property is set to 30 and that's the default value (the small reset arrow is not present)
5. Change the default value of this property in the resource script to something else (e.g. health = 100)
6. Look at the inspector for the resource instance, it tells me the property is set to 100 (lie!) and no reset to default arrow is present
7. Run the project, see that the value is still 30 (MRP .zip has a main scene that will helpfully print out the value, but just opening the resource file in an external text editor would work too to verify the value)
8. Reload the project
9. See that value of the property has been 30 all along in enemy1.tres

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Open main.tscn and follow the steps outlined above
[resource-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23757141/resource-issue.zip)

summary: Scripts in Resources fail to save properly

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1] ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7242) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.93 GiB memory ### Issue description When creating a script on a resource that is not stored in a separate .gd file, then after editing it and hitting CTRL+S, it will keep the unsaved indicator (the (\*) at the end of the filename). Trying to Close it will give the confirm dialog with (Save/Cancel/Discard), but neither selecting Save nor Cancel will actually save the changes to the script. While in this state (still open with (\*) indicator) if project is closed or "Reload Current Project" is selected then one will get stuck with the unsaved changes confirm dialog, and pressing "Save & Reload" will just make it re-appear again and again. Things that DO make the script save: using Save the script from the context menu of the script view or pressing the corresponding shortcut Ctrl+Alt+S. Or anytime the resource itself is saved, like selecting save from the inspector while the resource is open. ### Steps to reproduce 1. open a scene (for some reason necessary, issue does not happen if no scene has been opened since starting godot) 2. Create new Resource 3. Create a new GDScript object for the Resources script property 4. write something in there like `extends Resource` to cause a change in the script 5. hit Ctrl + S (the name in the open script view list should have a (*) next to it now) 6. Try various things like closing it in the script view or pressing Reload Current Project 7. When checking the resource file with an external text editor it can be seen that the changes to the script are not saved ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) The steps are easy to do from an empty project but one can also use the bug.zip from this issue: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101991#issuecomment-2613203826

summary: Clarify wording for mouse_entered and mouse_exited

description.text:
The documentation for `mouse_entered` and especially `mouse_exited` in `Control` are quite a mouthful:

> Emitted when the mouse cursor leaves the control's (and all child control's) visible area, that is not occluded behind other Controls or Windows, provided its mouse_filter lets the event reach it and regardless if it's currently focused or not.
> 
> Note: CanvasItem.z_index doesn't affect, which Control receives the signal.

Once I arrive at the last bit (`regardless if...`), it is quite unreadable for me, because I don't know which way everything is bracketed, so to speak. This PR is an attempt to improve that. Let me know what you think :)

Besides the clarification, I extended the Note regarding the z_index, because as the sentence was written, my reaction was: "Okay, so the z_index doesn't affect it. Why should it, resp. what _does_ affect it?". I think the Note came about because users where confused that the scene tree order is what's relevant, and not the z_index, so I stated this explicitly.

summary: VisibilityOnScreenEnabler2D Treats modulate values 0 and 1 as off-screen.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.5.1.stable
- Unknown for other versions.

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable (f62fdbde1) - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8157) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700H (20 threads) - 31.67 GiB memory

### Issue description

Perhaps this is not a bug, and is intentional. However, I thought it was worth bringing up just in case.

Panning down the "modulate" property of a node to 0 or 1 in alpha triggers not-visible, firing the _on_visible_on_screen_enabler_2d_screen_exited() signal of VisibilityOnScreenEnabler2D (VOSE2D). A value of 0 may compute, but a value of 1 seems strange, as that is a non-zero visibility. A value of 2 does not trigger this. This says to me that instead of cutting "visibility" at a hard-zero value, Godot may have a threshold of alpha value that counts as not visible. If it is reading 1/255 = 0.0039 and 2/255 = 0.0078, perhaps the threshold is somewhere around 0.005.

### Steps to reproduce

- Have a scene with an attached script, a VisibilityOnScreenEnabler2D (VOSE2D) node, an AnimationPlayer node, a Camera2D node, and two other nodes that can be seen in a running game, like two Sprite2D's with different images loaded.
- In AnimationPlayer, create a blinking animation that toggles the visibility true/false property of the whole scene rapidly on discrete update mode for a short time when damage is taken or some other repeatable scenario.
- Position the Camera2D to make both Sprite2D'f fully visible in-game.
- Connect VOSE2D's "_on_visible_on_screen_enabler_2d_screen_exited()" to the scene script and have it queue_free() **just one** of the Sprite2D's in its function.
- Load the game and take damage or trigger the blink animation. See that the one Sprite2D frees when the first not-visible frame activates. This is typical, expected functionality.
- Change the visibility track in the animation to Modulate, toggling from 0 to 255, still on discrete update mode. Notice that the Sprite2D is freed again. This could still be seen as expected functionality, debatable.
- Change the toggling from 0 to 1, so that it is now switching between 1 and 255. Notice that again, the Sprite2D frees. This does not seem like expected functionality, IMO, as an alpha of 1 is visible.
- Change the toggling from 1 to 2, so that it is now switching between 2 and 255. The Sprite2D should not free, and instead partake in the blinking animation normally with the rest of the scene.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Easily repeatable.

summary: AnimationMixer cannot merges/handles duplicated property tracks in the same animation

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5.stable Different behavior in 3.6 ### System information Godot v4.5.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8157) - AMD Ryzen 9 7940HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 63.21 GiB memory ### Issue description `AnimationPlayer` does not handle properties when a track is duplicated. The following example tries to move a sprite from (0, 0), to (250, 0), twice, over two tracks, with the second starting as the first completes. - Track 1 `Position`: - - 0.0s (0, 0) - - 0.5s (250, 0) - Track 2 `Position`: - - 0.5s (0, 0) - - 1.0s (250, 0) The expected behavior is for the sprite to move from (0, 0) to (250, 0) over 0.5 secs, then snap back to (0, 0) and repeat. However the sprite ends up with the movements combined (500, 0). In Godot 3.x, this method was supported, so long as the property changes did not overlap. It's not clear to me whether this is an intentional break in 4.x, and duplicate tracks are no longer supported, or whether this is a bug. The movement to (500, 0) seems unexpected in either case. If its intended for this method to be no longer supported, then duplicate tracks should be disallowed, and/or warnings logged, and docs should be updated accordingly. For further context, this method was being used in our project to "snap" something back to its original position and animate again elsewhere, reusing the object multiple times in the same animation player. This is achievable in 4.x by putting the "snap" position on the same track 0.001s after the end position instead, but this is particularly horrible to then manage as the values are on top of each other in the UI. Splitting tracks made it much easier to manage. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create an animation with a `position` track to move a Sprite2D. 2. Set the key-frames at (0, 0), and (250, 0) at 0s at 0.5secs respectively. 3. Create another track for the same property (e.g. by copying the track). 4. On the second track, set the key-frames at (0, 0), and (250, 0) at 0.5s at 1.0secs respectively. Run the animation and notice the sprite never return to (0, 0), and and up at (500, 0). ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [animation-tracks.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23734909/animation-tracks.zip)

summary: Update Blender importer to handle more Vertex Color options

description.text: Fixes #97953 On Blender 4.2, Blender changed its export options for Vertex Color from `export_colors` that was a true/false variable to `export_vertex_color` which is an enum with multiple values. This PR matches updates the Godot interface to better access these options, namely `MATERIAL`, `ACTIVE` and `NONE` For compatibility, if a boolean is read from a `.import` config file, it is processed by `handle_compatibility_options` where a `false` is mapped to `NONE` and a `true` is mapped to `MATERIAL` If a person uses a Blender version prior to 4.2, then selecting `NONE` will send `export_colors` a `false` value and any other option will send a `true` value. `MATERIAL` is default for fresh models. According to https://docs.blender.org/api/current/bpy.ops.export_scene.html , this will "Export vertex color when used by material."

summary: No system cursors on windows when using LibGodot

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.6 dev 4 (bd2ca13c6) and later (checked with bbe965432)

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (bd2ca13c6) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21033.3005) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.88 GiB memory

### Issue description

When compiling Godot as a library and calling it from a windows executable the mouse cursor is hidden when not using a custom image. Mouse events itself are processed (buttons are highlighted when moving the mouse).

After some research the most likely cause is this: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/bbe965432747f49ac647883c871a38ade26bdc4a/platform/windows/display_server_windows.cpp#L2984
According to the [windows docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/winuser/nf-winuser-loadcursora) NULL/nullptr should be used for loading system cursors. As far as I see hinstance is only set to something other than NULL/nullptr when using the LibGodot functions to create the engine, which is why passing that was not a problem before.

Passing nullptr does fix the issue, though I don't know enough to know whether this is the best fix.

### Steps to reproduce

Compile godot with "platform=windows library_type=shared_library" and use the functions in core/extension/libgodot.h in a windows executable.

N/A

summary: Add button to sort scripts by most recently opened

description.text:
Implements [#8125](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/8125) in the style of [#11656](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11656), proposed as an alternative to #83608.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29cf3323-21ea-4170-a5fa-ece1ade251fd

Notable changes from the other PR:
- Instead of an EditorSetting, this PR adds a toggle button similar to the "sort methods alphabetically" button present in the members overview below. The persistence of the button is recorded in a `.godot` config file, as opposed to the EditorSetting that the other button has, for the sake of ease-of-access and to avoid complication with the three (much more granular) "Sort Scripts By" options.[^1]
- This toggle reorders scripts/docs in reverse chronological order, with most recently opened at the top. For the sake of usability, this reordering only happens when the mouse moved outside of the scripts list, or if the mouse is already outside. In my testing, I've found this to be much more usable than doing so immediately or at a delay.
- Additionally the original sorting state of the list (along with any manual reordering) is restored when this button is untoggled.
- Adds a new `SortRecent.svg` file to mimic the style of the `Sort.svg` while showing a clock on the right rather than a list.

In my opinion, this implementation allows for a little more usability than the older PR, and I hope I'm not overstepping.

[^1]: In my testing, I also discovered a bug with the EditorSetting that the "sort methods alphabetically" button changes. The button changes the editor setting, but the editor setting does not change the button state, causing inconsistency in the interface until the button is recycled. I chose to make this PR not conform with that button's behaviour in the hope that this gets merged and vice versa can happen instead.

summary: GUI: Allow buttons to be pressed programmatically

description.text:
Currently, there's no simple, reliable, and complete way to programmatically press a button with `toggle_mode = false` (for `toggle_mode = true`, you can use the `button_pressed` property or its `set_pressed()` setter directly). Possible workarounds:

1. Manually emit the `pressed` signal. While this may work in simple cases, it ignores many factors: the `disabled`, `toggle_mode`, and `button_group` properties, the `_pressed()` and `_toggled()` virtual methods, and the presence of internal protected virtual methods `pressed()` and `toggled()` that are not accessible externally.
2. Use `Input.parse_input_event()` to simulate a button press using the `ui_accept` event or a mouse click. The problem is that this requires the button
   1. to have focus - you must `grab_focus()` to make `_gui_input()` working and ensure that `focus_mode` is set correctly, including ancestors, taking into account `focus_behavior_recursive`;
   2. to be clickable - you must ensure that `mouse_filter` is set correctly, including ancestors, taking into account `mouse_behavior_recursive`.
   3. Also, changing the current focus owner or handling input events can have unexpected side effects. Input events may be caught by another node or not be available at all (`Viewport.gui_disable_input`).

* See also https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3438.

summary: Remove extra `NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED` notifications in docks

description.text:
See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/113034#issuecomment-3566705258 Related: #113024 When upgrading docks to the new system, I first noticed that docks treated the `NOTIFICATION_{ENTER, EXIT}_TREE` like constructors and destructors, only being called once. When they used the `EditorBottomPanel`, this was valid, but now that they use the `EditorDockManager`, this is no longer true. Thus, any actions that must be done once, should be moved. In https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/113034#issuecomment-3566705258, the underlying bug was a case where `NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED` was used to know when the panel was enabled or disabled. This is valid if the notification only happened when both the enabled and visible, and disabled and not visible. However, due to an ordering issue with `EditorDockManager`, the `NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED(true)` was emitted when closing the dock, and `NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED(false)` was emitted when opening the dock, leading to incorrect assumptions. This PR swaps the order of operations so that the notification is only emitted correctly, to preserve assumptions. This has the benefit of removing unnecessary operations when opening and closing docks. | | `EditorDock::open()` | `EditorDock::close()` | | - | - | - | | Before: | NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED
NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE
NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED
NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE
NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED
NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED | NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE
NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED
NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE
NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED | | After: | NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE
NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE
NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED | NOTIFICATION_VISIBILITY_CHANGED
NOTIFICATION_EXIT_TREE
NOTIFICATION_ENTER_TREE |

summary: Show progress dialog when unzipping or duplicating project

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/guidelines/code_style.html
-->

Implements https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13641.

Adds a progress bar when importing, installing, or duplicating a project and puts those file operations on a background thread. They're not cancellable.

This required a custom directory copy function to support progress-reporting. I based it mostly on `DirAccess::copy_dir`, but simplified, and adapted for a two-stage copy (first recursively find all files to be able to get the total file count and then copy them, it's similarish to `EditorFileSystem::copy_directory`).

I've only tested this on Windows, so tests on other systems are welcome!

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.1 (didn't test other versions).


### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.5.1.stable - Vulkan (Forward+) - NVIDIA RXT 3060 ti

### Issue description

This bug apparently only happens when using Jolt Physics System:
Have a CharacterBody3D using MoveAndSlide(). Have an Area3D set at world origin (global position: 0, 0, 0).
Reparent the CharacterBody3D to some node near it, with KeepGlobalTransform set to true. Then Reparent it again to the Current Scene node, again with KeepGlobalTransform set to true. The Area3D that is at the world origin will trigger body entered even though the character is nowhere near the area 3D.


### Steps to reproduce

Set project Physics System to JOLT.
Create a CharacterBody3D with a script using MoveAndSlide(). Place the character far from the World Origin. 
Create another Node3D near the CharacterBody3D (this will be node you will reparent and unparent the character to).
Create an Area3D set at world origin (global position: 0, 0, 0), make its collider shape radius at least as big as the distance between CharacterBody3D and the Node3D it will be parented/unparented to.
Create a script that Reparents the CharacterBody3D to the Node3D with KeepGlobalTransform set to true when a key (Space in the MRP I provide below) is pressed, and Reparents it to the Current Scene node (with KeepGlobalTransform set to true) when another key is pressed (Left Ctrl  in the MRP I provide below). 

Using GD.Print for knowing when the area3D is triggered, we can see that when the CharacterBody3D is reparented from the Node3D back to the current scene node, it triggers the Area3D that is placed at world origin, even though the characterBody3D is never overlapping the area.
Calling Reparent() directly or deferred doesn't seem to make a difference for this issue.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

To test it: press Space bar to parent the CharacterBody3D to the other object (a RigidBody3D). Press left Ctrl to reparent it back to the current scene node. It's when reparenting to the current scene node that it will trigger the area 3D. The area3D is the big sphere positioned at world origin.


[reparent-bug-minimal.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23689804/reparent-bug-minimal.zip)

summary: Disabled tabs in TabBar are selectable with mouse

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.6dev4, since https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107440

### System information

Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8180) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10750H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads) - 15.82 GiB memory

### Issue description

Disabled tabs should not be selectable with the mouse, but they are.
This kind of defeats the entire purpose of disabled tabs.

- Fix: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/112935

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have a TabBar or TabContainer
2. Set a tab to be disabled
3. Click on it
4. It shouldn't select, but it does get selected. It gets set to the current tab and emits the related signals.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
